items.author: Fieldhouse Files
items.body_excerpt: Good evening and welcome back to Fieldhouse Files. We’re just two days into NBA free agency, and it’s been slower than normal — while still featuring several splash moves. Nothing was more surprising than the Celtics moving Jaylen Brown to the 76ers on the first full day of free…

items.author: Richard Deitsch
items.body_excerpt:
ESPN PR sends over that the Serena Williams-Maya Joint match averaged 1.8 million viewers (2:30-5 p.m. ET) with a peak of 2.1M between 4-4:15 p.m.

In the 18-49 demo:
Serena-Maya averaged 411,000 viewers. Peaked at 529K.

That's an interesting career decision.

items.title: Sources: The Sacramento Kings are signing Daeqwon Plowden to a 2-year, $5.1 million deal. After two-way deals with the Warriors, Hawks, and Kings, Plowden gets a standard NBA deal after averaging 10.8 points last season. Agent Drew Kelso of One Motive Sports will finalize the dea
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Sources: The Sacramento Kings are signing Daeqwon Plowden to a 2-year, $5.1 million deal. After two-way deals with the Warriors, Hawks, and Kings, Plowden gets a standard NBA deal after averaging 10.8 points last season. Agent Drew Kelso of One Motive Sports will finalize the deal.

media.title: What will Walker Kessler bring to the Lakers, and is he worth the price? (Gift Article)

items.title: Free agent guard Pat Spencer has agreed to a two-way NBA contract with the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Spencer's role grew across three seasons in Golden State, where he averaged 7.2 points and 18.6 minutes last season.

items.body_excerpt: Free agent guard Pat Spencer has agreed to a two-way NBA contract with the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Spencer's role grew across three seasons in Golden State, where he averaged 7.2 points and 18.6 minutes last season.
